description:
## What
Check the domain_blocks table on every inbound activity before processing. Suspended domains: drop the activity (return 200 to not reveal the block). Silenced domains: process but filter posts from public/federated timelines. Also check domain blocks in the outbound delivery worker: skip delivery to suspended domains. Implement the check at the DB lookup layer so new handlers do not need to remember to check.

## Why
Domain blocks are a safety and moderation primitive. Without enforcement at the inbox layer, suspended domains can still inject content. Part of sub-epic 8-B federation lifecycle.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: read Mastodon domain block behavior (mastodon.social admin API docs); check what HTTP status mastodon.social returns for activities from blocked domains; document the three severity levels: suspend (drop everything), silence (filter from public timelines), and reject_media (strip attachments).
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: add domain_blocks table migration (domain TEXT PRIMARY KEY, severity TEXT, reject_media BOOLEAN, created_at INTEGER); add check_domain_block(domain) function returning block level; call this at top of shared inbox handler before dispatching; for silence: add domain column to remote_posts or filter via JOIN on remote_accounts; add domain check in delivery worker before making HTTP request; implement reject_media flag (discard media_attachments before storing remote_post).
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write integration tests; seed domain_blocks with suspended and silenced entries; dispatch activities from each; assert suspended domain activity is dropped and returns 200; assert silenced domain post does not appear in GET /api/v1/timelines/public; assert delivery worker skips suspended domain inbox URLs.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: Mastodon source code behavior (github.com/mastodon/mastodon); test assertions compare timeline query results against seeded DB state (not the filter code itself).
- Test approach: integration test with in-memory SQLite; seed domain_blocks rows; POST activity to inbox; query timeline; assert presence/absence; delivery worker test: seed queued delivery to suspended domain, run worker, assert no HTTP call made via wiremock with no registered route.

## Defensive programming checklist
- Check domain block before any DB writes or activity processing
- Return 200 (not 403) for suspended domains to avoid leaking block information
- Domain extraction: parse from actor_id URI hostname, not from user-supplied field
- No .unwrap() in inbox handler path
- Apply reject_media BEFORE storing the remote_post row
- Domain block check must be a single SQL lookup not N+1
title: Domain block enforcement in inbox router and delivery worker
